economic_notes: Two government business enterprises accounted for using modified equity method: God's Lake Smoke Shop and Restaurant, and God's Lake Contracting Inc. First Nation is economically dependent on ISC and Health Canada funding. Large construction projects in progress including a new school ($42.2M) and nursing station ($20.3M).
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - auditors were not provided with evidence to support several invoices in accounts payable and accrued liabilities, and could not confirm cutoff of expenses. Fiscal year is 2020, not 2024. Auditor report dated May 6, 2025, which is over five years after year-end March 31, 2020 - highly unusual. Two GBEs (Smoke Shop/Restaurant and Contracting Inc.) accounted for under modified equity method. Significant construction in progress of $68.8M including a major school project ($42.2M) and nursing station ($20.3M). Large increase in deferred revenue from $1.85M to $16.4M driven by school and infrastructure projects. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $44,193,250 + FNIH $8,413,476 + CMHC $366,672 + ESDC $904,072 + Province of Manitoba $1,035,671 + Ottawa Trust Fund portion in restricted cash = $54,913,141 minus eliminations portion attributable to government transfers; reported here as sum of all identifiable government revenue lines excluding band generated revenue, other revenue, and rental income = 44193250+8413476+366672+904072+1035671 = 54913141, but after eliminations osr adjusted accordingly.
